title: Stelis robustior
intro: Stelis robustior, also known as Stelis, is a plant species in the orchid family native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Stelis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Stelis robustior is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. Stelis robustior is classified under the family Orchidaceae and further categorized under the subfamily Epidendroideae. It belongs to the tribe Epidendreae and is a part of the subtribe Steliinae. The species is native to Central and South America and is found in various regions, including Costa Rica, Panama, and Ecuador. Stelis robustior is an epiphytic orchid, which grows on other plants without harming them.
